Experts warn that prolonged exposure to distressing news and social media can result in brain fatigue, difficulty concentrating, and disrupted sleep patterns. The emotional toll of doomscrolling can leave individuals feeling anxious, disheartened, and drained.

To combat the negative effects of doomscrolling, various apps have emerged that offer engaging and productive alternatives. These applications provide users with opportunities to connect with the world in a more meaningful way, steering them away from mindless scrolling. For instance, Radio Garden allows users to listen to over 25,000 live radio stations from around the globe, fostering a sense of global connectivity without the pitfalls of social media.

While traditional methods like reading a book or going for a walk are effective in breaking the doomscrolling cycle, these apps serve as convenient options for those looking to fill short breaks with enriching content. By utilizing these tools, users can reclaim their time and mental energy, transforming their smartphone usage from a source of stress into a platform for learning and connection.
social_text: 64% of Americans report doomscrolling, impacting mental health and focus. Apps like Radio Garden offer alternatives, connecting users to 25,000+ live radio stations globally, promoting healthier content consumption.

title: The leaderboard “you can’t game,” funded by the companies it ranks
summary:
Arena, previously known as LM Arena, has rapidly established itself as a leading public leaderboard for frontier large language models (LLMs) within just seven months of its inception. Originating from a UC Berkeley PhD research project, the startup has achieved a remarkable valuation of $1.7 billion. This platform plays a crucial role in shaping the competitive landscape of AI by influencing funding decisions, product launches, and public relations strategies among major players in the industry, including OpenAI, Google, and Anthropic.

The co-founders, Anastasios Angelopoulos and Wei-Lin Chiang, emphasize the importance of creating a neutral benchmark that is resistant to manipulation, unlike traditional static benchmarks. Arena's innovative approach to ranking LLMs focuses on structural neutrality, which aims to provide a fair assessment of AI models' capabilities. Currently, the platform highlights Claude as a leading model in legal and medical applications, showcasing its effectiveness in specialized use cases.

As Arena continues to evolve, it is expanding its benchmarking capabilities beyond chat models to include agents, coding, and real-world tasks through a new enterprise product. This strategic expansion positions Arena as a pivotal player in the AI landscape, providing essential insights and benchmarks that can guide both developers and investors in navigating the rapidly changing AI ecosystem.
social_text: Arena, valued at $1.7B, is the leading public leaderboard for frontier LLMs, backed by OpenAI, Google, and Anthropic. Its structural neutrality prevents gaming, with Claude excelling in legal and medical tasks. New enterprise products are on the horizon.

title: This startup wants to make enterprise software look more like a prompt
summary:
Eragon, a startup founded by Josh Sirota, aims to revolutionize enterprise software by eliminating traditional interfaces in favor of a prompt-based system. With a recent funding round raising $12 million at a $100 million post-money valuation, the company is positioned to challenge established software giants like Salesforce and Jira. Sirota's vision is rooted in the belief that conventional software interfaces, characterized by buttons and menus, are outdated. Instead, he proposes that future business interactions will be conducted through a more intuitive, language-based interface powered by large language models (LLMs).

The startup's approach is not just theoretical; it is backed by a team of skilled professionals, including PhD students from prestigious institutions like Berkeley and MIT. This technical expertise is crucial as Eragon seeks to develop a comprehensive suite of business software that can seamlessly integrate into modern workflows. Investors, including notable names like Arielle Zuckerberg and Axiom Partners, recognize the potential for Eragon to become a vital tool for teams looking to enhance their operational efficiency and decision-making processes.

Sirota's journey to founding Eragon was marked by a significant career shift, moving from corporate roles at Oracle and Salesforce to launching his own venture. His experience in the tech industry has instilled confidence in investors regarding his ability to execute this ambitious vision. As Eragon continues to develop its platform, the startup is poised to redefine how businesses interact with software, potentially making traditional interfaces a relic of the past.
social_text: Eragon, founded by Josh Sirota, raised $12M at a $100M valuation to create an AI-driven prompt-based enterprise software system, aiming to replace traditional interfaces with LLM technology for seamless business operations.

title: Facebook launches a new monetization program to attract popular creators from TikTok, YouTube
summary:
Facebook has introduced a new initiative called 'Creator Fast Track' aimed at attracting popular creators from platforms like TikTok and YouTube. This program is designed to provide creators with guaranteed pay and enhanced content reach, facilitating their transition to Facebook without the need to start from scratch. By offering financial incentives, Facebook hopes to entice creators who have already established significant followings elsewhere to begin sharing their content on its platform. The company has reported a substantial increase in its payments to creators, totaling nearly $3 billion in 2025, marking a 35% rise from the previous year and the highest annual figure to date.

The 'Creator Fast Track' program allows creators with at least 100,000 followers on other platforms to earn $1,000 per month, while those with over one million followers can earn up to $3,000 monthly. This initiative not only provides financial support but also grants creators immediate access to Facebook's monetization tools, bypassing the usual follower count requirements. This strategic move is aimed at alleviating concerns from creators about the challenges of building a community on Facebook, as highlighted by Yair Livne, VP of Creator Product at Facebook.

In addition to the initial three-month financial support, Facebook has committed to continuing to boost the reach of creators until they establish a solid audience on the platform. This approach reflects Facebook's recognition of the competitive landscape in social media and its desire to retain and attract top talent. By addressing creators' concerns and providing them with the necessary tools and incentives, Facebook is positioning itself as a viable platform for content creation and monetization, potentially reshaping the dynamics of social media engagement among creators.
social_text: Facebook's new Creator Fast Track program offers creators $1,000/month for 100k+ followers and $3,000/month for 1M+, aiming to attract top talent from TikTok and YouTube while boosting reach and monetization tools.

title: Three reasons Netflix shares are headed for a breakout, according to Citi
summary:
Citi has resumed coverage of Netflix with a bullish outlook, assigning a buy rating and a price target of $115, suggesting a potential upside of over 20% from its recent close. Analyst Jason Bazinet highlights three key factors that could drive Netflix's stock performance. Firstly, the absence of major M&A activity, particularly the failed acquisition of Warner Bros. Discovery, allows Netflix to revise its operating income margin guidance upwards, potentially reaching 32% for the full year 2026. This adjustment reflects a more favorable financial outlook for the streaming giant.

Secondly, Bazinet anticipates that Netflix will likely implement a price increase in October 2026, a move previously thought to be unlikely due to regulatory scrutiny surrounding M&A activities. The analyst expresses confidence in Netflix's pricing power, suggesting that the company can successfully raise subscription prices without significant pushback from consumers. This strategic decision could further enhance revenue streams and bolster investor confidence.

Lastly, with a stronger cash position, Netflix is expected to increase its share repurchase activities, which could provide an additional 10% boost to its stock value. While there are concerns regarding advertising revenue growth, which Bazinet estimates at $1.5 billion—lower than the $2 billion consensus—he believes these risks are manageable and will not overshadow the positive catalysts. Overall, Citi's analysis positions Netflix as a resilient leader in the streaming industry for the foreseeable future.
social_text: Citi resumes coverage on Netflix with a buy rating and a $115 target, citing potential 32% operating margins, upcoming price hikes, and increased share repurchases as key growth drivers despite ad revenue risks.

title: This tech stock is primed for a big move higher. How to trade it using options
summary:
The current market landscape has shown signs of potential recovery after a prolonged dry spell since February. As equities attempt to establish a solid base, traders are advised to remain cautious due to the Cboe Volatility Index hovering in the 20s. This environment necessitates a controlled approach to trading volume and frequency, even as select high-quality stocks begin to emerge as viable targets. Among these, MongoDB stands out, presenting a promising opportunity for traders looking to capitalize on a potential upward movement.

Technical analysis reveals that MongoDB is exhibiting a classic mean-reversion setup, which is particularly appealing in the current market context. Two key indicators are being monitored to time the entry: the Fast MACD and the Relative Strength Index (RSI). The Fast MACD has indicated a bullish crossover since March 9, demonstrating sustained momentum. Meanwhile, the RSI, which had previously dipped below the critical 30 threshold, has shown signs of recovery, breaking back above this level on March 4, suggesting that buyers are regaining control over the stock.

To take advantage of this anticipated rebound, a 265-270 bull call spread is proposed, allowing traders to gain upside exposure while managing risk effectively. This strategy involves a modest investment of around $2.50 per spread, with the potential for a balanced risk-reward scenario. By risking $1,000 for a chance to earn an equivalent profit, traders can engage with MongoDB without overexposing their capital, making this setup an attractive option in a cautious trading environment.
social_text: MongoDB shows a bullish setup with a MACD crossover since March 9 and an RSI recovery above 30. A 265-270 bull call spread at $2.50 offers a balanced risk-reward with $1,000 risk for $1,000 profit potential.

title: Microsoft hires the team of Sequioa-backed AI collaboration platform, Cove
summary:
Microsoft has acquired the team behind Cove, a startup known for its AI-powered collaboration platform, which is set to shut down its services. Founded in late 2023 by former Google Maps engineers Stephen Chau, Andy Szybalski, and Mike Chu, Cove raised $6 million in seed funding from notable investors including Sequoia Capital and Elad Gil. The platform offered an innovative infinite whiteboard that utilized AI to assist users in generating task-related blocks, enhancing collaboration through a more flexible interface compared to traditional chat-based AI tools.

Cove's unique features included the ability to integrate a built-in browser, PDFs, and images, allowing users to provide context for AI-generated content such as cards, tables, and lists. The startup positioned itself as a competitor to established platforms like Miro and TLDraw, aiming to redefine collaborative work through AI. However, with the acquisition by Microsoft, the Cove service will cease operations on April 1, 2024, and all user data will be deleted, although users will receive refunds for March subscriptions.

In a statement, the Cove team expressed their excitement about continuing their mission at Microsoft AI, where they believe they can pursue a larger vision for AI collaboration. Microsoft has previously integrated AI capabilities into its products, such as adding Copilot to its Whiteboard application in 2023. The integration of Cove's technology into Microsoft's ecosystem remains to be detailed, as inquiries to Microsoft have yet to receive a response, leaving users and industry observers eager for more information on future developments.
social_text: Microsoft acquires Cove, a Sequoia-backed AI collaboration startup, shutting down its service on April 1, 2024. The team, known for innovative whiteboard features, will enhance Microsoft's AI capabilities.

title: AI’s ‘boys’ club’ could widen the wealth gap for women, says Rana el Kaliouby
summary:
At the recent SXSW conference, AI scientist and entrepreneur Rana el Kaliouby expressed her concerns regarding the lack of diversity in the AI sector, which she believes could exacerbate economic disparities for women in technology. El Kaliouby, who has a notable background as the co-founder of Affectiva and now serves as a general partner at Blue Tulip Ventures, highlighted the alarming trend of AI being perceived as a 'boys' club.' She emphasized that this perception is not merely a myth but a reality that could hinder women's economic opportunities in the tech landscape.

El Kaliouby pointed out that the current investment landscape is skewed, with a significant majority of funding going to male-led startups. She noted that at her venture firm, three out of four investments are directed towards startups with women CEOs, underscoring her commitment to supporting female entrepreneurs. However, she stressed that investing in women founders is not just a matter of equity; it is crucial for the overall health and innovation of the tech industry. Without adequate representation and support for women in AI, the sector risks perpetuating existing inequalities.

The conversation around diversity has become increasingly contentious, especially following the rollback of Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion (DEI) initiatives during the Trump administration. El Kaliouby warned that if women continue to be excluded from founding companies, receiving funding, and participating in investment decisions, the economic gap will only widen in the coming years. She urged the industry to recognize the importance of diversity not only as a moral imperative but as a strategic necessity for fostering innovation and economic growth in the AI sector.
social_text: Rana el Kaliouby warns that the AI sector's lack of diversity could widen the economic gap for women. At SXSW, she highlighted that 75% of her investments are in women-led startups, emphasizing the need for equitable opportunities.

title: Oura enters India’s smart ring market with the Ring 4
summary:
Oura, the Finnish smart ring manufacturer, is making its debut in the Indian market with the launch of its Ring 4. This entry comes at a time when the Indian smart ring market is still in its infancy, facing challenges such as declining shipments and price sensitivity due to the influx of lower-cost alternatives. Oura's pricing strategy positions the Ring 4 between ₹28,900 and ₹39,900, with an additional monthly membership fee of ₹599. This pricing is competitive when compared to local rival Ultrahuman, whose products range from ₹28,499 to ₹42,990. Despite the potential for growth, the market has seen a 30.6% decline in shipments in 2025, indicating a struggle to maintain momentum in a niche segment that has yet to gain widespread consumer awareness.

The smart ring segment in India is characterized by limited competition and a lack of a robust ecosystem to foster innovation and consumer engagement. According to IDC, Ultrahuman currently leads the market with a 30.4% share, followed by Gabit at 18.3%. The overall market dynamics have been affected by a decrease in average selling prices, which fell by 8.7% to $159.7. Vikas Sharma, a senior market analyst at IDC, notes that while there was initial excitement around smart rings as a tech trend, sustained growth has been hampered by muted marketing efforts and the prioritization of larger markets by vendors.

Oura aims to differentiate itself by positioning the Ring 4 as a premium health device that offers more than just basic functionality. The integration of a subscription service that provides personalized insights into sleep, activity, and recovery is a strategic move to attract health-conscious consumers. This approach contrasts with many local competitors who primarily focus on price. As Oura enters this challenging landscape, its success will depend on its ability to educate consumers about the benefits of smart rings and to carve out a significant niche in a market that is still developing.
social_text: Oura launches its Ring 4 in India, priced between ₹28,900 and ₹39,900, competing against Ultrahuman's offerings. The smart ring market faces a 30.6% shipment decline, indicating challenges in consumer awareness and competition.

title: World launches tool to verify humans behind AI shopping agents
summary:
World, co-founded by Sam Altman, has introduced a new verification tool aimed at enhancing the integrity of AI-driven shopping agents. This initiative comes in response to the growing concerns over AI-generated content and the potential for fraud in online transactions. The tool, named AgentKit, is designed to facilitate 'agentic commerce,' where AI programs autonomously browse and make purchases on behalf of users. As the use of AI agents becomes more prevalent, the need for a reliable verification system has become critical to ensure that real humans are behind these transactions.

AgentKit operates on the foundation of World ID, a secure identification system that utilizes biometric data for verification. The most secure version of World ID is generated through an iris scan using the World Orb device, which converts the iris into a unique encrypted digital code. This code not only verifies the user's identity but also allows seamless access to a range of services within the Tools for Humanity ecosystem. By integrating World ID with AgentKit, commercial websites can confirm the authenticity of the purchasing agent, thereby mitigating risks associated with automated transactions.

Additionally, AgentKit is compatible with the x402 protocol, a blockchain-based payment system developed by Coinbase and Cloudflare. This protocol enables automated transactions between computer programs without the need for human oversight at every step. By registering their AI agents with their World ID, users can ensure that their transactions are secure and legitimate, paving the way for a more trustworthy online shopping experience. This innovative approach addresses the dual challenges of convenience and security in the rapidly evolving landscape of AI commerce.
social_text: World's new AgentKit tool enables secure verification of AI shopping agents using World ID, derived from iris scans. This innovation combats fraud in automated transactions, enhancing trust in agentic commerce.

title: What time is today's Fed meeting?
summary:
The Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) is concluding its meeting today, where it evaluates the economic landscape and considers adjustments to the federal funds rate. This meeting is part of a series of scheduled gatherings aimed at shaping monetary policy in response to economic conditions. The FOMC's decisions are crucial as they influence interest rates, which in turn affect borrowing costs, consumer spending, and overall economic growth. The next scheduled meeting will take place on March 17-18, 2026, marking the second meeting of the year.

At the conclusion of today's meeting, the FOMC is expected to announce its policy decisions at 2 p.m. Eastern time, followed by a news conference led by Fed Chairman Jerome Powell at 2:30 p.m. These events are significant as they provide insights into the Fed's monetary policy direction and economic outlook. The Fed's commitment to maintaining the federal funds rate within the target range of 3.5% to 3.75% reflects its cautious approach to balancing employment goals and inflation control.

While the CME Group's FedWatch tool indicates a minimal chance of a rate cut, the FOMC emphasizes the importance of assessing incoming data and evolving economic conditions before making any adjustments. Investors and consumers alike should stay informed about these meetings, as they can significantly impact financial decisions and market trends. Preparing for potential changes in monetary policy is essential for navigating the economic landscape effectively.
social_text: The FOMC meeting concludes today, with a policy decision expected at 2 p.m. ET. The Fed is likely to maintain the federal funds rate at 3.5%-3.75%, with a less than 1% chance of a rate cut, emphasizing economic stability.

title: Docusign beats Q4 estimates but analysts stay on the sidelines on execution concerns
summary:
DocuSign (NASDAQ:DOCU) has reported impressive fourth quarter results for fiscal 2026, surpassing analyst expectations in both revenue and earnings. The company achieved adjusted earnings per share of $1.01, exceeding the consensus estimate of $0.95, and generated revenue of $836.9 million, slightly above the forecast of $828.2 million. Additionally, billings for the quarter reached $1.02 billion, surpassing expectations of approximately $997.7 million. This positive performance led to a 3% increase in the company's stock price, reflecting investor confidence in its growth trajectory.

CEO Allan Thygesen emphasized the role of DocuSign's AI-driven Identity and Access Management (IAM) platform in driving this growth, which has established a strong market presence. In 2026, customers utilizing the IAM platform contributed over $350 million in annual recurring revenue (ARR). The company also reported record highs in operating margin and free cash flow, showcasing its operational efficiency. Analysts from Wedbush noted the robust demand for DocuSign's products, particularly those enhanced with AI capabilities, and expressed optimism about the company's guidance for fiscal 2027.

Looking ahead, DocuSign anticipates ARR growth of 8.25% to 8.75% for fiscal 2027, with IAM expected to account for approximately 18% of total ARR. The company has also seen significant growth in its international revenue, which now makes up 30% of total revenue, and an increase in its customer base to 1.82 million. The number of customers spending over $300,000 annually has risen to 1,205, indicating strong demand for its services. Despite these positive indicators, some analysts remain cautious about execution concerns, advising a wait-and-see approach before making investment decisions.
social_text: DocuSign reported Q4 2026 earnings of $1.01 EPS on $836.9M revenue, beating estimates. ARR grew to $3.272B, with IAM driving $350M. Analysts cautious despite strong guidance for fiscal 2027.

title: Google to allow AI opt-out to ease UK competition concerns
summary:
Google has announced plans to implement new search controls that will allow websites to opt out of its generative AI features. This move comes in response to concerns raised by the UK's Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) regarding Google's dominance in the search services market. With over 90% of UK search queries being conducted through Google, the CMA has designated the company as having 'strategic market status,' which enables regulatory intervention to promote effective competition. The proposed changes aim to address the CMA's concerns while also improving user experience by reducing intrusive pop-ups that prompt users to change their default search engine.

The CMA's ongoing efforts to enhance competition in the digital landscape include measures that allow publishers to opt out of having their content used for AI Overviews or for training AI models. This is particularly significant as news websites and publishers have reported a decline in click-through rates due to users increasingly relying on AI-generated overviews. Google has expressed concerns that some of the CMA's proposed requirements could lead to 'disproportionate consequences' for users and businesses, potentially hindering its ability to innovate and invest in the UK market.

In light of these developments, Google has committed to working collaboratively with the CMA to find practical solutions that will benefit all stakeholders involved, including users, publishers, and businesses. The company's proactive approach reflects its recognition of the need to balance regulatory compliance with the ongoing evolution of AI technologies and their impact on the search landscape. As the situation unfolds, the implications for competition in the digital space will be closely monitored by industry observers and stakeholders alike.
social_text: Google is developing new search controls allowing websites to opt out of generative AI features to address UK competition concerns. The CMA's measures aim to enhance competition and transparency in search results, impacting over 90% of UK queries.

title: Morgan Stanley is Bullish on Vistra Corp (VST) Here’s Why
summary:
Morgan Stanley has expressed a bullish outlook on Vistra Corp. (NYSE:VST), highlighting its potential as a strong investment opportunity in the context of the growing demand for AI infrastructure. In a recent research note, the firm assigned a Buy rating to Vistra with a price target of $215. The analysts emphasized that the demand for computing power is expected to continue outpacing supply, driven by advancements in AI technologies, particularly large language models. This trend is anticipated to create significant economic value, positioning companies that provide essential technologies, including power generation and AI infrastructure, for substantial growth.

The report also pointed out that the shift in focus among bitcoin miners towards AI, along with neocloud companies securing large prepayments for capital expenditures to develop high-performance computing data centers, further underscores the importance of AI infrastructure. Morgan Stanley believes that Vistra Corp., along with other companies in the power and utilities sector such as GE Vernova and Bloom Energy, stands to benefit from this trend. With a robust generation portfolio of 44,000 megawatts, Vistra is well-positioned to capitalize on the increasing demand for energy solutions that support AI advancements.

While Morgan Stanley acknowledges the potential of Vistra as an investment, they also caution that certain AI stocks may offer greater upside potential with less downside risk. Investors are encouraged to consider the broader landscape of AI-related stocks, particularly those that may benefit from current economic trends, including tariffs and the onshoring movement. Overall, Vistra Corp. is highlighted as a key player in the evolving energy market, particularly in relation to the burgeoning AI sector.
social_text: Morgan Stanley rates Vistra Corp. (NYSE:VST) as a Buy with a $215 target, citing strong demand for AI infrastructure and a 44,000 MW generation portfolio. Companies like GE Vernova and Bloom Energy are also poised to benefit.

title: Hedge the risk that small-cap stocks will suffer from rising rates with this options strategy
summary:
Recent market analyses indicate a concerning shift in the macroeconomic landscape, primarily driven by rising oil prices due to geopolitical tensions, particularly the ongoing conflict in Iran. This situation is exacerbated by a slowdown in economic growth and a resurgence of inflationary pressures, which are beginning to weigh heavily on small-cap stocks. The February jobs report revealed a significant downturn, with payrolls declining by 92,000 and an uptick in unemployment rates, suggesting that economic momentum is faltering. As financial conditions tighten due to increasing bond yields, the outlook for small-cap stocks, which are particularly sensitive to these economic shifts, appears increasingly precarious.

The Russell 2000 index, which tracks small-cap stocks, is showing signs of vulnerability as macroeconomic pressures mount. The recent rise in the 10-year yield indicates a tightening of financial conditions at a time when growth is already weakening. Small-cap stocks have been underperforming compared to their large-cap counterparts, reflecting their heightened sensitivity to domestic economic fluctuations. Analysts warn that if yields continue to rise, the Russell 2000 could face a significant downside risk, potentially dropping to the $230 level, which marks the lower end of its recent trading range.

The combination of slowing growth and rising costs presents a particularly challenging environment for small-cap companies, many of which lack the pricing power to maintain profit margins in the face of increasing input costs. As labor markets show signs of deterioration and inflation persists, the outlook for small-cap stocks becomes increasingly grim. Investors are advised to consider options strategies to hedge against the risks associated with this volatile market environment, particularly as the economic landscape continues to evolve rapidly.
social_text: Rising oil prices and a February jobs report showing a 92,000 payroll decline signal a fragile economy. Small-cap stocks face heightened risks as bond yields rise, with the Russell 2000 potentially dropping to $230 amid tightening conditions.

title: Disney trades at a historically low valuation. Sarat Sethi sees a great buying opportunity
summary:
Disney's recent earnings report highlighted a strong performance from its parks and experiences unit, which generated $3.31 billion in profit and $10.01 billion in revenue, surpassing analyst expectations. This segment's success is particularly significant as it represents the only division to exceed both top and bottom line estimates, indicating robust demand and effective pricing strategies. The company anticipates that this unit will continue to drive high-single-digit growth in operating income for the fiscal year, reflecting a positive outlook for Disney's core experiences business.

In addition to the parks and experiences segment, Disney is witnessing a turnaround in its streaming profitability, with margins projected to rise from 5% to 10% this year. This improvement is crucial as the company navigates challenges such as rising gasoline prices and the ongoing impact of the Iran oil crisis on consumer spending. Furthermore, Disney's valuation remains historically low, with a price-earnings ratio below 15, making it an attractive investment opportunity for those looking to capitalize on its potential growth.

The upcoming leadership transition, with Josh D'Amaro set to take over as CEO from Bob Iger, underscores the company's commitment to its parks and experiences division, which is seen as vital for future growth. As Disney prepares to increase its dividend and share buybacks, investors are encouraged by the strategic moves aimed at enhancing shareholder value. Despite recent struggles in its stock performance, the combination of strong earnings in key segments and a focus on returning capital to shareholders positions Disney favorably for future success.
social_text: Disney's parks and experiences unit posted $3.31B profit and $10.01B revenue, exceeding estimates. Streaming margins are set to rise to 10%. Valuation is historically low with a P/E ratio under 15, making it a strong buy.

title: Nvidia shares are rising before its big AI conference. Here's what Wall Street expects to hear
summary:
Nvidia's shares have seen a notable increase as anticipation builds for its upcoming annual GTC conference, where CEO Jensen Huang is set to deliver a keynote address. Investors are particularly eager for insights into the sustainability of the artificial intelligence spending boom and the company's next generation of processors. The stock rose approximately 2% ahead of the event, reflecting growing confidence in Nvidia's ability to maintain its leadership in AI infrastructure amidst increasing competition from rivals like Advanced Micro Devices and custom AI chip manufacturers.

Analysts are closely monitoring the semiconductor sector, particularly the spending habits of hyperscalers on AI hardware, which have been robust over the past two years. Morgan Stanley has reaffirmed Nvidia as their top pick in semiconductors, suggesting that the upcoming conference could clarify Nvidia's long-term market share amidst rising competition. Meanwhile, Wells Fargo has noted that Nvidia's stock has underperformed relative to the broader semiconductor sector, which could be a point of concern for investors.

The GTC conference is seen as a potential catalyst for Nvidia's stock, especially as analysts have high expectations for the company's future earnings, with estimates for 2027 hovering around $13 per share. However, there is a call for clearer long-term targets from Nvidia, as competitors have already laid out multiyear outlooks. The outcome of the conference could significantly influence investor sentiment and Nvidia's market position moving forward.
social_text: Nvidia shares rose 2% ahead of the GTC conference, where CEO Jensen Huang will address AI spending sustainability. Analysts expect 2027 earnings at $13/share, raising questions about long-term market share against AMD and others.

title: Marquis says over 672,000 people had personal and financial data stolen in ransomware attack
summary:
Marquis, a fintech company based in Plano, Texas, has revealed that a significant ransomware attack in August 2025 compromised the personal and financial data of over 672,000 individuals. This breach, which has now been fully disclosed, primarily affected customers of various banks that utilize Marquis' services for data analysis and visualization. The company is in the process of notifying those impacted, with more than half of the affected individuals residing in Texas. The stolen data includes sensitive information such as names, dates of birth, postal addresses, bank account details, debit and credit card numbers, and Social Security numbers.

The attack has raised serious concerns regarding cybersecurity within the fintech sector, particularly as Marquis has taken legal action against its firewall provider, SonicWall. The lawsuit alleges that SonicWall's security shortcomings allowed hackers to access critical firewall configuration files, which were subsequently exploited to breach Marquis' network. This incident highlights the vulnerabilities that can exist within third-party security systems and the potential repercussions for companies that rely on them.

As the investigation continues, the implications of this breach extend beyond the immediate financial data theft. It underscores the urgent need for enhanced cybersecurity measures within the fintech industry, as well as the importance of accountability among service providers. The fallout from this incident may lead to increased scrutiny of data protection practices and regulatory responses aimed at safeguarding consumer information in the digital age.
social_text: Marquis reports that over 672,000 individuals had their personal and financial data stolen in a ransomware attack. The breach, affecting primarily Texas residents, included sensitive information like Social Security numbers and bank details.

title: Russians caught stealing personal data from Ukrainians with new advanced iPhone hacking tools
summary:
Recent cybersecurity research has unveiled a sophisticated hacking campaign targeting iPhone users in Ukraine, attributed to a group known as UNC6353, which is believed to have ties to the Russian government. This campaign employs a new hacking toolkit named Darksword, designed to steal personal data and potentially cryptocurrency from its victims. The analysis conducted by Google and security firms iVerify and Lookout indicates that this operation is part of a broader trend of advanced cyberattacks aimed at Ukrainian citizens amidst ongoing geopolitical tensions. The Darksword toolkit follows the earlier discovery of another hacking tool called Coruna, which was initially developed for surveillance purposes by the U.S. defense contractor L3Harris. This suggests a troubling evolution in the sophistication and availability of spyware, particularly targeting vulnerable populations in conflict zones.

The emergence of Darksword highlights the increasing accessibility of advanced hacking tools that were once thought to be exclusive to government agencies or elite cybercriminals. Researchers noted that while Darksword is currently focused on Ukraine, its capabilities raise concerns about the potential for similar tools to be deployed against a wider audience in the future. The targeted nature of these attacks indicates a strategic choice by the hackers, possibly reflecting a desire to limit exposure while still achieving their objectives. This development underscores the ongoing cybersecurity challenges faced by individuals and organizations in Ukraine, as they navigate a landscape fraught with threats from state-sponsored actors.

As the situation evolves, the implications of these cyberattacks extend beyond immediate data theft. They pose significant risks to national security and the integrity of personal information for millions of users. The international community must remain vigilant and responsive to these threats, as the tools and tactics employed by cybercriminals continue to advance. The findings from this research serve as a critical reminder of the need for enhanced cybersecurity measures and awareness, particularly in regions experiencing conflict and instability.
social_text: A new hacking toolkit named Darksword, linked to Russian hackers, targets iPhone users in Ukraine, stealing personal data and cryptocurrency. This follows the earlier Coruna toolkit, highlighting advanced cyber threats in conflict zones.

title: DOD says Anthropic’s ‘red lines’ make it an ‘unacceptable risk to national security’
summary:
The U.S. Department of Defense (DOD) has officially labeled Anthropic as an 'unacceptable risk to national security,' marking a significant escalation in the ongoing legal battle between the AI lab and the Pentagon. This classification stems from concerns that Anthropic's self-imposed 'red lines' regarding the use of its technology could lead the company to disable its systems during critical military operations. The DOD's argument, presented in a detailed 40-page court filing, emphasizes the potential risks associated with allowing a private entity to dictate the operational parameters of military technology, particularly in high-stakes scenarios such as warfare.

Anthropic, which secured a $200 million contract with the Pentagon last summer, has been vocal about its reservations regarding the application of its AI systems, particularly in contexts involving mass surveillance and lethal decision-making. The company has argued that its technology is not yet suitable for such uses, and it has sought legal recourse to challenge the DOD's classification, claiming that it infringes on its First Amendment rights and is ideologically motivated. The legal dispute has garnered attention from various tech organizations and civil rights groups, many of whom have filed amicus briefs in support of Anthropic, arguing that the DOD could have opted to terminate the contract instead of imposing such a label.

As the situation unfolds, a hearing is scheduled for next Tuesday to address Anthropic's request for a preliminary injunction against the DOD's classification. The outcome of this case could have significant implications for the relationship between the military and private tech companies, particularly in the rapidly evolving field of artificial intelligence. The DOD's stance raises critical questions about the balance of power between government agencies and private enterprises in the context of national security and technological innovation.
social_text: The DOD has labeled Anthropic an 'unacceptable risk to national security,' citing concerns over its 'red lines' potentially impacting military operations. Anthropic's $200M contract is now under scrutiny as it challenges the DOD's classification in court.

title: MacBook Neo, AirPods Max 2, iPhone 17e, and everything else Apple announced this month
summary:
This month, Apple made significant strides in its product lineup with the introduction of several new devices aimed at various market segments. The announcements began on March 2 with the unveiling of the iPhone 17e, a budget-friendly smartphone priced at $599. This model features the A19 chip and offers 256 GB of storage, doubling the entry-level storage of its predecessor, the iPhone 16e. Notably, the iPhone 17e includes MagSafe and Qi2 capabilities, allowing for wireless charging up to 15 W, along with a 48-megapixel camera that mirrors the previous model's specifications.

Following the iPhone reveal, Apple continued to impress with the launch of the M5 MacBook Air and new MacBook Pro models, showcasing the advanced M5 Pro and M5 Max chips. These new laptops are designed to enhance performance and efficiency, catering to both casual users and professionals. Additionally, Apple introduced the MacBook Neo, a more affordable option that runs on a chip similar to those found in the iPad and iPhone, further expanding its reach in the budget laptop market.

The excitement didn't stop there, as Apple surprised fans with the announcement of the AirPods Max 2, the much-anticipated successor to its premium headphones. This release marks a significant update to Apple's audio offerings, which have been a key part of its ecosystem. Overall, these announcements reflect Apple's commitment to innovation and its strategy to cater to a diverse range of consumers, from budget-conscious buyers to high-end users seeking the latest technology.
social_text: Apple's March announcements include the iPhone 17e at $599 with A19 chip and 256 GB storage, M5 MacBook Air, MacBook Neo, and AirPods Max 2, enhancing its product range for diverse consumers.

title: Carnival Earnings Anxiety: The Good, Bad, and Ugly
summary:
Carnival Corp., the leading cruise line operator globally, is set to release its fiscal first-quarter earnings report on March 29. Investors are keenly awaiting this update, as the company has experienced significant stock volatility but has managed to outperform the market over the past year, with a 25% increase. Over the last three years, Carnival's stock has nearly tripled, showcasing a resilient recovery post-pandemic. However, the stock has faced challenges recently, with a 20% decline over the past six months and an 18% drop in 2026, raising concerns among investors about the upcoming earnings report.

Analysts predict that Carnival will report earnings of $0.18 per share for the last fiscal quarter, a notable improvement from the $0.13 per share reported a year earlier. This expectation is bolstered by Carnival's historical performance, where it has consistently exceeded market expectations in the past ten fiscal quarters. For instance, in the previous quarter, the company surprised analysts with a 46% better-than-expected performance, indicating a trend of positive surprises that investors hope will continue.

As Carnival prepares for this critical earnings announcement, the market will be closely monitoring the results to gauge the company's financial health and future prospects. The upcoming report will not only reflect Carnival's operational efficiency but also its ability to navigate the ongoing challenges in the travel and leisure sector. Investors are hopeful that the company will maintain its track record of exceeding expectations, which could further stabilize its stock performance in the coming months.
social_text: Carnival Corp. is set to report fiscal Q1 earnings on March 29, with analysts predicting $0.18 EPS, up from $0.13 last year. The stock has seen a 25% rise over the past year but is down 20% in the last six months.

title: Why Guardant Health’s (GH) 2026 Screening Story Reinforced Piper Sandler’s Bullish View
summary:
Guardant Health, Inc. (NASDAQ:GH) has garnered attention from analysts and investors alike, particularly after its recent performance and optimistic projections for 2026. As of March 13, 2026, the company holds a Moderate Buy consensus from 23 analysts, with 21 Buy ratings, one Hold, and one Sell. The average 12-month price target is set at $122.57, indicating a potential upside of approximately 43% from its current share price of $85.49. This bullish sentiment is bolstered by a strong revenue report for Q4 2025, where Guardant Health achieved $281.3 million in revenue, marking a 39% year-over-year increase. Notably, oncology revenue rose by 30%, while screening revenue reached $35.1 million, supporting the company's growth narrative for the upcoming year.

The positive outlook for Guardant Health was further reinforced during recent healthcare conferences, where management elaborated on strategic partnerships and market expansion opportunities. The collaboration with Quest Diagnostics was highlighted as a significant commercial driver for the company's Shield product. Management noted that 90% of Shield users had not undergone screening in the previous five years, suggesting a substantial market opportunity for expanding screening services rather than merely competing in an existing market. Additionally, potential FDA-related advancements for Guardant360 and the exclusion of Reveal Ultra from 2026 guidance due to reimbursement uncertainties were discussed as catalysts for future growth.

Piper Sandler analyst David Westenberg has maintained a Buy rating on Guardant Health, recently raising his price target from $120 to $130. This adjustment reflects the company's strong quarterly performance and the promising outlook for its screening initiatives. With expectations of total revenue growth between 27% and 30% for 2026, Guardant Health is positioned to capitalize on its innovative products and strategic partnerships, making it a compelling investment opportunity in the healthcare sector.
social_text: Guardant Health's Q4 2025 revenue hit $281.3M (+39% YoY), with oncology revenue at $189.9M. Piper Sandler raised the price target to $130, signaling strong growth potential for 2026 amid strategic partnerships.

title: Why TD Cowen Turned Bullish on Rivian Automotive (RIVN) Ahead of the R2 Launch
summary:
TD Cowen has recently upgraded Rivian Automotive, Inc. (NASDAQ:RIVN) from Hold to Buy, raising the price target from $17 to $20. This bullish outlook is primarily driven by the anticipated launch of Rivian's R2 platform, which is expected to significantly increase the company's production volume. The R2 is positioned as a more affordable alternative to Rivian's current R1 models, with potential annual demand estimates ranging from 212,000 to 335,000 units. This strategic move is crucial for Rivian as it seeks to expand its market share in the competitive electric vehicle sector.

The R2 lineup was officially disclosed on March 12, 2026, featuring a launch variant priced at $57,990, with additional trims and an entry-level configuration expected to start at $45,000. This pricing strategy aims to attract a broader customer base and enhance Rivian's appeal in the growing electric vehicle market. Rivian's recent operational performance also supports this optimism, as the company reported delivering 42,247 vehicles in 2025 and achieving a consolidated gross profit of $144 million, a significant turnaround from a gross loss of $1.2 billion in 2024.

Looking ahead, Rivian has set ambitious delivery targets for 2026, forecasting between 62,000 to 67,000 vehicle deliveries. The launch of the R2 is seen as a pivotal moment for the company, potentially transforming its financial trajectory and solidifying its position in the electric vehicle landscape. While Rivian's growth potential is acknowledged, analysts suggest that other sectors, particularly AI, may present greater investment opportunities with lower risks.
social_text: TD Cowen upgraded Rivian (RIVN) to Buy, raising the price target to $20, driven by the upcoming R2 launch, expected to boost demand to 335,000 units annually. Rivian aims for 62,000-67,000 deliveries in 2026.
